---
title: "NH<sub>3</sub> Emission Factor Shiny App"
author: Chih-Yu Hung
output: html_document
---

## Overview

This project contains a Shiny app for estimating monthly NH<sub>3</sub> emission factors from manure temperature and optional monthly NH<sub>3</sub> emissions from TAN.

The app is based on the temperature-correction relationship documented in [NH3_EF_correction_notes.md](NH3_EF_correction_notes.md).

## Core Equation

For each month, the app calculates:

`EF_T = EFTref * CT^(T - Tref)`

Where:

- `T` is the monthly average manure temperature in `deg C`
- `Tref` is the reference temperature in `deg C`
- `EFTref` is the emission factor at `Tref`, in percent
- `CT` is the animal-specific temperature correction coefficient

The annual emission factor is the simple arithmetic mean of the 12 monthly values.

## Optional NH<sub>3</sub> Emissions

If the user enters `Monthly TAN (kg N)`, the app also calculates:

`Monthly NH3 emission = Monthly TAN * EF / 100`

Output units:

- `Monthly TAN`: `kg N`
- `NH<sub>3</sub> emission`: `kg NH<sub>3</sub>-N`

The same monthly TAN value is applied to all 12 months.

## Animal Defaults

The app includes the following built-in animal defaults:

| Animal type | CT | Default EFTref (%) |
|---|---:|---:|
| DAIRY_CATTLE | 1.056 | 12.2 |
| BEEF_CATTLE | 1.065 | 10.0 |
| OTHER_CATTLE | 1.062 | 7.6 |
| BUFFALO_BEEF | 1.067 | 13.4 |
| SHEEP | 1.049 | 25.0 |
| PIG | 1.036 | 29.6 |

`EFTref` is auto-filled from the animal type and remains editable.

## App Features

- Animal-type dropdown with animal-specific `CT`
- Editable `Tref` and `EFTref`
- Twelve monthly manure temperature inputs from January to December
- `Plot` button to trigger calculations
- Monthly line chart of EF with manure temperature overlay
- Annual average EF label on the chart
- Optional NH<sub>3</sub> emission calculation from monthly TAN
- Downloadable CSV output

## Files

- [app.R](app.R): main Shiny application
- [NH3_EF_correction_notes.md](NH3_EF_correction_notes.md): source notes for the calculation logic

## Run The App

Open R or RStudio in this folder and run:

```r
shiny::runApp()
```

Or run the file directly:

```r
source("app.R")
```

## Notes

- The app uses a simple 12-month average for annual EF.
- The chart shows EF as a solid line and manure temperature as a dashed line.
- The downloaded CSV uses plain-text column names for compatibility, including units where relevant.
